Source database schema verified; columns match requirement
Source database schema verified; columns derivable from genre, track, and invoice_items tables
Source database schema verified; columns derivable from invoices and invoice_items tables
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM artists; executed on source Chinook database
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT AlbumId) FROM albums WHERE ArtistId=90; (Iron Maiden)
SELECT COUNT(TrackId) FROM tracks WHERE AlbumId IN (SELECT AlbumId FROM albums WHERE ArtistId=90);
SELECT SUM(Milliseconds)/60000.0 FROM tracks WHERE AlbumId IN (SELECT AlbumId FROM albums WHERE ArtistId=90);
SELECT g.Name FROM genres g JOIN tracks t ON g.GenreId=t.GenreId JOIN invoice_items ii ON t.TrackId=ii.TrackId GROUP BY g.GenreId ORDER BY SUM(ii.UnitPrice*ii.Quantity) DESC LIMIT 1;
Top genre (Rock) revenue calculated from invoice_items; SUM(UnitPrice*Quantity)
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT strftime('%Y-%m', InvoiceDate)) FROM invoices;
https://github.com/lerocha/chinook-database - Well-known sample database for SQL education and ORM testing

privileged_info_summary: The Chinook database is a real, publicly available sample database containing digitized music store data (artists, albums, tracks from iTunes) plus fictitious customer and randomly-generated sales data. Database contains 275 artists, 60 distinct months of sales data, and $2,328.60 total revenue. Rock is the top genre by revenue ($826.65). Iron Maiden has 21 albums and 213 tracks totaling 1,197.41 minutes. The task requires creating three reporting tables (artist_summary, genre_revenue, monthly_sales) in a new SQLite database (chinook_reports.db) derived from the source Chinook database.
